_README: Allowlist for i18n-lint.mjs — declares every file containing dynamic `t(variable)` call sites and the set of keys those variables may resolve to at runtime. `dynamicCallSites` is a `"file": expectedCount` map: count-based (rather than per-line) so it doesn't churn on every edit, while still catching a NEW dynamic call sneaking into an already-allowlisted file (count mismatch). Each key that dynamic code paths can construct must appear somewhere in `referencedKeys` — the groups there are just for readability, the linter flattens them. If a declared key isn't in en-US.json, i18n-lint fails loud — the dynamic path would fall back to the literal string at runtime, which is almost always a bug. Refresh after adding/removing dynamic sites via `node scripts/i18n-lint.mjs --list-dynamic`.
